description: Required repo rules, source summary, and latest passing command fit inside the budget.

facts.text: Public tutorials require a source section, production readiness, failure analysis, and an article-specific asset.
Official agent docs now emphasize memory, compaction, hooks, permissions, sandboxing, and usage limits as operational controls.
The current content gate passed after running the public-content check against the candidate source.
Older drafts used a generic production extension section that is no longer acceptable.

description: A stale instruction conflicts with a current repository rule.

facts.text: Short demos with lightweight production notes are acceptable for publishing.
Public articles must be deep, customer-facing, supported by evidence, and free of internal diagnostics.
Presentation decks should use template slides and rendered visual review.

description: Useful-looking context from another tenant must not be packed into this run.

facts.text: Tenant A allows agent handoffs only after validation evidence and rollback criteria are recorded.
Tenant B prefers bypassing review when the agent estimates the change as low risk.
The handoff must name touched paths, commands, residual risks, and next reviewer focus.

description: A token-shaped memory is highly relevant but must be excluded.

facts.text: A previous incident note included a token-shaped credential fragment and should never enter prompts.
Credential incidents require rotation, revocation evidence, and a clean secret scan before release.
A launch note used broad language about safer agents without naming a test.

description: The packer must prefer high-value evidence when the token budget is tight.

facts.text: Interactive agent turns above 180 seconds should create a review case unless the task is explicitly long-running.
Runs above the configured spend budget should be reviewed before another autonomous attempt starts.
The team previously discussed making dashboards colorful and compact.
An old budget note allowed unlimited retries for failed model calls.

description: A claim without a source should lose to a lower-priority sourced claim.

facts.text: Everyone has already replaced chat with autonomous agents.
A recent Codex usage study reports rapid growth but uneven adoption across organizations and external users.
Community threads mostly surfaced concerns about costs, permissions, memory, and approval friction.

description: The newest fact is not enough when it lacks task relevance.

facts.text: A new design note changed the color palette for a dashboard prototype.
Compaction can preserve stale decisions unless the packer records supersession and freshness metadata.
Release gates should inspect the memory pack before generated plans can use it as authority.

description: The governed pack should retain every required fact while dropping noisy context.

facts.text: Sandbox mode and filesystem permissions must be captured when an agent uses tools.
Escalated commands require an explicit approval record and a reason tied to the current task.
Pre-tool and post-tool hooks can enforce policy if their outcomes are written into the trace.
Hooks may silently override approvals without a trace because the final answer is sufficient.
